1: a clear bright human head/mind on the left linked by thin lines to fresh information nodes that progressively fade, blur and dissolve into grey static on the right — fresh context decaying into stale noise
2: a chain of nine connected links, each only slightly cracked at 95 percent, the small flaws compounding so the final link is badly frayed and broken — reliability multiplying down, not adding
3: a tall stack of document lines where the top edge and bottom edge glow crisp in the accent color while the entire middle section fades into faint grey — the middle gets lost
4: a small figure proudly planting a victory flag on top of a bridge that is only half built, with the unfinished span trailing off into thin sketch lines — declaring victory early
5: a sea wall being built to the height of the single tallest wave on the horizon rather than the small average ripples — planning around the ceiling
6: a hand paused and hovering just above a button, with a branching plan diagram forming in a thought bubble first — think before you touch
7: several parallel reasoning paths running side by side and converging into one shared answer point, like votes meeting — many chains, not one
8: a decision tree that forks widely at the very first node, exploring several branches before one path is committed — branch when the first step matters
9: a person fussing over a tiny ornate clockwork contraption beside one large plain powerful engine that clearly wins — clever scaffolding versus raw compute
10: a clean path that reaches a bullseye target and then keeps going, overshooting past it and curving off into a wrong turn — extra thinking past the answer
11: a figure reaching upward but capped by a hard horizontal ceiling line labeled by its height alone — the answer can rise no higher than retrieval
12: a balloon held by a thin tether to a heavy anchor yet still drifting sideways a little — grounding reduces drift but does not eliminate it
13: one true key glowing in the accent color surrounded by many almost-identical near-miss keys that look right but are subtly wrong — relevant beats plentiful near-misses
14: a humble old index card and magnifying glass on exact words quietly outperforming a soft fuzzy neural blob beside it — keyword still carries weight
15: a tidy vertical hierarchy of stacked memory tiers of different sizes versus one single oversized empty box — memory is a layered system
16: three sharp precise tools standing clean and ready beside a cluttered drawer overflowing with thirty dull identical tools — narrow beats general
17: a soft fluid organic shape in the center held neatly inside rigid geometric brackets and rails at its edges — model in the middle, code at the boundaries
18: transparent pipework full of visible gauges and trace lines feeding into a steering wheel — you can only release control you can trace
19: one large unstable block splitting cleanly into several small steady stacked blocks rather than inflating bigger — decompose instead of supersize
20: a ladder of fixes where a hand reaches first for the lowest, simplest rung instead of the towering machine above — the cheapest fix first
21: a single tool with a clear legible label tag attached, sitting beside an unmarked mystery tool — a tool is only as usable as it is legible
22: a dense wall of prose lines crossed out and replaced by one clean simple worked example diagram — show, don't tell
23: a confidence gauge with its needle pinned hard at full while the actual arrow it fired clearly misses the target — certainty is not evidence
24: an agent at a fork in the road raising a single question-mark flag instead of confidently charging down one wrong path — surface ambiguity
25: one big smooth bar that looks high and healthy, with a hidden dip in one segment revealed underneath it — averages hide the weak segment
26: a single eyeball trying to judge a huge growing pile of outputs, melting under the scale, beside an unused measuring ruler — vibes don't scale
27: a person with a magnifying glass actually reading raw rows of data while others walk past ignoring them — look at your data
28: a balance scale tipped by the sheer length of one long answer outweighing a short substantive one — the judge reacts to length
29: an arrow stuck dead-center in a gauge that is being gamed while the real structure it was meant to measure quietly crumbles behind it — the metric becomes the goal
30: a patched crack held shut by a small lock, versus an identical crack with no lock silently reopening — fixed bugs regress without a test
31: three overlapping circles labeled only by distinct icons — private data, untrusted content, an exit arrow — with a warning glow where all three intersect — the lethal trifecta
32: a flowing stream of identical plain tokens where one is secretly an attacker's instruction, completely indistinguishable, wearing no badge — tokens don't wear badges
33: an agent holding a ring of the owner's keys while a shadowy hand quietly points it toward the wrong door — the confused deputy
34: a clean privileged planner figure behind a protective glass barrier, orchestrating while a pool of poisoned data stays sealed on the other side — quarantine untrusted tokens
35: a contained burst of energy fully boxed inside thick sandbox walls so the blast cannot spread — sandbox the blast radius
36: a simple clean straight pipeline running beside an over-engineered tangled looping agent, with the simple one clearly chosen — don't build an agent when a workflow will do
37: a tiered cascade of steps where a small cheap model handles most cases and only a rare hard case escalates up to a large model — cascade before you escalate
38: a row of agents multiplying, each one dropping another coin onto a growing stack of cost — the multi-agent tax
39: a system architecture diagram of connected boxes that mirrors the shape of an org chart of teams above it — architecture mirrors the org chart
40: a retry arrow looping back twice into an action, but the action carries a single 'stamped once' mark so it only takes effect one time — retries demand idempotency
41: a circuit breaker switch being flipped open to cut off a downstream node that is already sparking and failing — trip the breaker
42: a vast bank of machines handling the easy bulk of work while one tiny lone human is left to wrestle the single hardest leftover piece — the ironies of automation
43: a person with eyes closed obediently following a glowing dial, ignoring the contradicting evidence plainly in front of them — automation bias
44: an autonomy slider with several notch settings, its handle being matched against a separate rising scale of stakes — match the level to the stakes
45: a single mode toggle switch caught ambiguously between states with a question mark, causing confusion — mind the mode
46: two relay runners passing a baton, the baton slipping in the open gap between their hands — the handoff is the hard part
47: a trust meter filling up gradually alongside a rising track-record graph, step by step — trust is calibrated, not granted
48: one real open exit door beside a fake door merely painted onto a solid wall — without a clean exit, one gets fabricated
49: the same figure split by a dividing wall into a creator on one side and a judge on the other, kept apart — the author shouldn't grade its own work
50: a single fact card with a visible unbroken thread trailing all the way back to a labeled origin source — preserve provenance
